allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
security: remove exposed credentials and add environment template

- Remove config.env from repository tracking
- Add .env.example with placeholder values for all required environment variables
- Update .gitignore with comprehensive patterns for security files, logs, and build artifacts
- Prevent future credential exposure with enhanced gitignore rules

BREAKING CHANGE: config.env is no longer tracked. Copy .env.example to config.env and fill in your credentials.

Security Notice: All credentials previously in config.env must be rotated immediately:
- MongoDB Atlas password
- Cloudinary API keys  
- Gmail app password
- Stripe API keys
- JWT secret key
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
chore: initialize Node.js project with metadata

- Add comprehensive project description
- Set package name to asd-healthcare-api
- Add keywords for discoverability \(asd, autism, healthcare, api\)
- Change license from ISC to MIT for open-source compatibility
- Add Node.js engine requirements \(>=18.0.0\)
- Add start script for production deployment
- Organize scripts in logical order \(start, dev, test\)

This establishes the project foundation with proper metadata for npm registry
and open-source distribution.
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
chore: add EditorConfig for consistent coding style

- Configure UTF-8 charset for all files
- Set LF line endings for cross-platform compatibility
- Enforce 2-space indentation for JavaScript and JSON
- Add final newline and trim trailing whitespace
- Configure Markdown exception for trailing whitespace
- Set tab indentation for Makefiles

EditorConfig ensures consistent code formatting across different
editors and IDEs, improving team collaboration and code quality.
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
docs: add initial README with project overview

- Add project description and motivation
- List key features \(user management, healthcare services, AI integration\)
- Document tech stack with versions
- Add Getting Started section with prerequisites
- Include installation instructions
- Add MIT license and work-in-progress status

This provides essential documentation for developers getting started
with the project and establishes the foundation for comprehensive docs.
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --title "docs: add comprehensive visual diagrams \(ERD, UML, use case\)" --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## 📊 Overview

This PR adds comprehensive visual documentation to improve developer onboarding and system understanding.

## 🎯 Changes

### Mermaid Diagrams Added

- **Entity Relationship Diagram \(ERD\)**: Complete database schema showing 11 entities and their relationships
- **Use Case Diagram**: System functionality with 3 actors \(Parent, Doctor, Admin\) and 37 use cases across 8 categories
- **UML Class Diagram**: All 13 Mongoose models with attributes, methods, and relationships
- **Sequence Diagrams** \(3 workflows\):
  - Authentication Flow \(signup → verification → login → password reset\)
  - Appointment Booking Flow \(create → browse → book → confirm/cancel\)
  - Payment Processing Flow \(web/mobile checkout → webhooks → order creation\)

### Documentation Files

#### `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` \(700+ lines\)
Comprehensive system architecture documentation including:
- System goals and layered architecture
- Technology stack breakdown
- 7 detailed system components
- Data model relationships
- Key workflows with diagram references
- Security architecture \(JWT, rate limiting, encryption\)
- API design principles
- Deployment architecture
- Performance optimizations \(35+ database indexes\)

#### `docs/diagrams/README.md`
Instructions for viewing, editing, and generating PNG images from Mermaid diagrams

### README.md Enhancements

#### Architecture & Database Design Section
- Embedded Mermaid ERD preview
- Links to full diagrams \(ERD, Use Case, Class Diagram\)
- System architecture overview
- Sequence diagram references

#### Code Examples Section \(400+ lines\)
Complete request/response examples with expected HTTP status codes:

**Authentication Flow**
- Parent signup with email verification
- Email verification with JWT token response

**Appointment Booking**
- Browse available appointments with doctor details
- Book specific appointment slot

**AI Screening Integration**
- Get screening questions
- Submit answers for prediction with risk assessment

**Payment Processing**
- Create Stripe checkout session \(web\)
- View payment history

**Error Handling**
- Validation error examples
- Authentication error examples

#### Developer Guide Section \(250+ lines\)
- Quick start for contributors
- Environment setup guides:
  - MongoDB Atlas \(with screenshots workflow\)
  - Cloudinary configuration
  - Stripe setup with webhook testing via Stripe CLI
  - Gmail App Password setup
  - FastAPI AI service local development
- Project structure documentation
- Development workflow with conventional commits
- Troubleshooting guide:
  - Database connection issues
  - JWT token errors
  - CORS configuration
  - Docker port conflicts
  - Stripe webhook failures
- Links to additional resources

## ✅ Benefits

1. **Faster Onboarding**: Visual diagrams reduce learning curve for new contributors
2. **Better Understanding**: ERD and UML diagrams clarify system architecture
3. **Clear Workflows**: Sequence diagrams document critical business processes
4. **Self-Service**: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide reduces support burden
5. **Production-Ready**: Professional documentation expected in enterprise projects
6. **Version Controlled**: Mermaid format allows diagrams to be diffed in git
7. **GitHub Native**: Mermaid renders automatically in GitHub markdown

## 📸 Preview

### ERD Preview
```mermaid
erDiagram
    Parent ||--o{ Child : has
    Parent ||--o{ Order : places
    Parent ||--o{ Appointment : books
    Doctor ||--o{ Appointment : provides
    Doctor ||--o{ Session : conducts
    Session ||--o{ SessionReview : has
```

### Code Example Preview
```javascript
// Authentication with expected response
const response = await fetch\(''http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/signup'', {
  method: ''POST'',
  headers: { ''Content-Type'': ''application/json'' },
  body: JSON.stringify\({ userName: "John Doe", email: "john@example.com", ... }\)
}\);

// Expected Response \(201 Created\)
{
  "status": "success",
  "message": "Check your email to verify account",
  "data": { "userId": "65a7b3cf48f2a123456789a", ... }
}
```

## 🔍 Testing

- [x] All Mermaid diagrams render correctly in GitHub
- [x] README.md table of contents updated
- [x] All documentation links are valid
- [x] Code examples follow project conventions
- [x] No broken image references
- [x] Mermaid syntax validated on https://mermaid.live

## 📚 Related Documentation

- [Full ERD]\(https://github.com/3bwahab/asd-healthcare-platform/blob/docs/add-diagrams/docs/diagrams/erd.mmd\)
- [Use Case Diagram]\(https://github.com/3bwahab/asd-healthcare-platform/blob/docs/add-diagrams/docs/diagrams/use-case.mmd\)
- [Class Diagram]\(https://github.com/3bwahab/asd-healthcare-platform/blob/docs/add-diagrams/docs/diagrams/class-diagram.mmd\)
- [Architecture Documentation]\(https://github.com/3bwahab/asd-healthcare-platform/blob/docs/add-diagrams/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\)

## 🎓 Impact

This PR transforms the project from "good code" to "production-ready with excellent developer experience". The visual documentation and comprehensive examples make the codebase accessible to:
- New contributors
- Frontend developers integrating with the API
- DevOps engineers deploying the system
- Security auditors reviewing architecture
- Product managers understanding workflows
